name: OVHcloud Cloud Infrastructure API
what_it_does: European cloud infrastructure provider with REST API — enabling enterprises and developers to provision dedicated servers, VPS, public cloud (OpenStack-compatible), managed Kubernetes, object storage, databases, and CDN across 40+ data centers globally, with European sovereign cloud positioning and GDPR compliance from French jurisdiction.
use_cases: Provisioning OVHcloud VPS and dedicated servers via API for automated infrastructure deployment
Managing OVHcloud Public Cloud instances via OpenStack-compatible API for cloud compute automation
Configuring OVHcloud managed Kubernetes clusters via API for k8s infrastructure management
Managing OVHcloud domains and DNS via API for DNS automation pipelines
Querying OVHcloud bandwidth and billing metrics via API for infrastructure cost monitoring
not_for: Teams preferring AWS/Azure/GCP ecosystem (OVHcloud has different service naming and APIs)
Enterprise managed services equivalent to AWS RDS or Lambda serverless (limited managed services)
Simple developer PaaS deployment (use Railway, Render, or Fly.io)
best_when: A European enterprise or privacy-focused team needs GDPR-compliant, sovereign cloud infrastructure at competitive pricing — with dedicated servers, public cloud compute, and managed Kubernetes under EU data jurisdiction.
avoid_when: You need AWS-equivalent managed services breadth, prefer US data residency, or need developer-friendly PaaS.

auth.notes: Three-part auth: Application Key + Application Secret + Consumer Key. API keys created at api.ovh.com/createToken. Scoped access per endpoint and HTTP method (read vs write per route). No OAuth2 as such — OVHcloud's own 3-key model. OpenStack tokens for Public Cloud (Keystone auth). HMAC-SHA1 signature required for each request (timestamp + method + URL + body). No webhooks (poll for status). SDKs for Python, Go, PHP, Node.js, C#.

pricing.paid_tiers: VPS from €3.99/month; Dedicated servers from €39/month; Public Cloud hourly; Managed Kubernetes from €0.01/hour/node
pricing.notes: French company (Roubaix). Founded 1999 by Octave Klaba. €1.3B revenue (2022). 40+ data centers across Europe, North America, Asia Pacific. Third largest web hosting company globally. 1.6 million customers. Strong in European market. Sovereign Cloud certification (SWIPO). HDS (French healthcare data hosting). SecNumCloud qualification (ANSSI French national cybersecurity). OpenStack-based Public Cloud. ISO27001, ISO27017, ISO27018. Politically independent cloud — not US hyperscaler. Growing enterprise segment with GCP and VMware partnerships.

performance.rate_limits: 60 requests per hour per consumer key (Management API)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with 3-key auth (App Key + App Secret + Consumer Key). HMAC-SHA1 signature per request — non-standard pattern requiring timestamp. SDKs for 5 languages. 60 req/hour rate limit on management API. No webhooks — must poll. OpenStack for Public Cloud (different auth). French sovereign cloud certifications.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Resource reads idempotent by ID. Domain reads idempotent. Power operations idempotent for same desired state. Resource creation not idempotent — check by name. Billing reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: 3-key auth (App Key + App Secret + Consumer Key) + HMAC-SHA1 signature — complex auth implementation
Consumer keys expire unless set to unlimited — manage token lifecycle
60 req/hour rate limit on management API — very restrictive for automation
OpenStack API for Public Cloud uses different auth (Keystone tokens) from management API
No webhooks — must poll for server and task status

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, ISO27001, HDS, SecNumCloud compliant. 3-key auth with per-route scoping — good granularity. HMAC-SHA1 signature provides request integrity. SecNumCloud qualification (ANSSI) — French government-grade security. HDS for French healthcare hosting. French jurisdiction — EU sovereign cloud. Consumer key expiry manageable. OpenStack authentication for Public Cloud resources.
